preamble.summary: Judah delivers his plea on Benjamin's behalf, offering himself as a slave. Joseph reveals his identity. He weeps, assures them God sent him ahead, instructs them to bring Jacob.
preamble.remarkable: Judah's speech — the offer of self-substitution — transforms him from the brother who proposed selling Joseph into the patriarch willing to enslave himself for Benjamin.
preamble.friction: Jubilees abbreviates Judah's speech compared to Genesis 44:18-34.

verses.text_reference: And Judah came near and said: 'O my lord, let thy servant speak.'
verses.rendering: Judah stepped forward and said: Please, my lord, let your servant speak. Do not be angry, for you are equal to Pharaoh.

verses.text_reference: 'We have a father, an old man, and a child of his old age.'
verses.rendering: We told you: We have an aged father and a young boy born in his old age. The boy's brother is dead, and he alone is left of his mother's children.

verses.text_reference: 'The lad cannot leave his father.'
verses.rendering: We said: The boy cannot leave his father. If he leaves, his father will die.

verses.text_reference: 'Thou saidst: Bring him down.'
verses.rendering: But you told us: Unless your youngest brother comes, you shall not see my face again.

verses.text_reference: And our father said: 'Go again and buy food.'
verses.rendering: We went back and told our father. He said: Go back and buy a little food.

verses.text_reference: 'We cannot go without our youngest brother.'
verses.rendering: We said: We cannot go unless our youngest brother goes with us.

verses.text_reference: And our father said: 'My wife bare me two sons; one went away.'
verses.rendering: Your servant my father said: You know my wife bore me two sons. One went away, and I have not seen him since.

verses.text_reference: 'If ye take this one and harm befall him, ye will bring my grey hairs to the grave.'
verses.rendering: If you take this one too and something happens to him, you will bring my grey head down to the grave in sorrow.

verses.text_reference: 'His soul is bound up with the lad's soul.'
verses.rendering: If I go back without the boy — and his life is bound up with the boy's life — he will die when he sees the boy is not with us.

verses.text_reference: 'I became surety for the lad.'
verses.rendering: Your servant pledged himself as surety. If I do not bring him back, I will bear the blame forever.

verses.text_reference: 'Let me remain instead of the boy.'
verses.rendering: Let your servant remain as a slave in place of the boy. Let the boy go home. How could I face my father without him?
translator_notes: Judah's self-substitution is the moral climax of the entire Joseph narrative.

verses.text_reference: And Joseph could not restrain himself.
verses.rendering: Joseph could no longer contain himself. He cried out: Send everyone away! No one remained when Joseph revealed himself.

verses.text_reference: And he wept aloud and said: 'I am Joseph! Doth my father yet live?'
verses.rendering: He wept so loudly that the Egyptians heard. He said to his brothers: I am Joseph! Is my father still alive?
translator_notes: 'I am Joseph' — one of the most dramatic moments in all of Scripture.

verses.text_reference: And his brethren could not answer him.
verses.rendering: His brothers could not answer him, for they were overwhelmed with shock.

verses.text_reference: And Joseph said: 'I am Joseph your brother, whom ye sold into Egypt.'
verses.rendering: Joseph said: I am your brother Joseph, whom you sold into Egypt. Do not be grieved, because God sent me ahead of you to preserve life.

verses.text_reference: 'God sent me before you to preserve you as a remnant.'
verses.rendering: The famine has been in the land for two years, and five years remain. God sent me to preserve you as a remnant.

verses.text_reference: 'It was not you who sent me here, but God.'
verses.rendering: It was not you who sent me here — it was God. He has made me ruler over all Egypt.

verses.text_reference: 'Go up to my father and tell him.'
verses.rendering: Hurry! Go to my father and tell him: Your son Joseph says — God has made me lord over Egypt. Come down to me.

verses.text_reference: 'Thou shalt dwell in Goshen.'
verses.rendering: You will live in the land of Goshen and be near me — you, your children, your flocks, and everything you own.

verses.text_reference: And Joseph wept on Benjamin's neck, and kissed all his brethren.
verses.rendering: Joseph threw his arms around Benjamin and wept, and Benjamin wept on his neck. He kissed all his brothers and wept over them.

verses.text_reference: And Pharaoh said: 'Bring your father and come to me.'
verses.rendering: Pharaoh was pleased and said: Tell your brothers — bring your father and families. I will give you the best of Egypt.
